questions.q: What is the primary purpose of planning in an organisation?
questions.answer: The primary purpose of planning is to achieve the organisation's objectives by selecting the most effective course of action.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: Planning involves _______________ alternative courses of action.
What are the characteristics of planning? Explain any four.
questions.answer: Planning has several characteristics: (i) Planning is a primary function of management, (ii) Planning is a continuous process, (iii) Planning is a mental process, and (iv) Planning is a pervasive function.

questions.q: State whether the statement is True or False: Planning is a one-time activity.
Explain the difference between single-use plans and standing plans.
questions.answer: Single-use plans are designed to achieve a specific goal and are not repeated, whereas standing plans are recurring and can be used multiple times to achieve routine objectives.

questions.q: What are the types of plans? Explain any two.
questions.answer: The two types of plans are: (i) Strategic plans: These are long-term plans that define the overall direction of the organisation, and (ii) Tactical plans: These are short-term plans that outline the specific actions to be taken to achieve the strategic plans.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The process of planning involves _______________ the future.
Explain the importance of planning in an organisation.
questions.answer: Planning is essential for an organisation as it helps to achieve its objectives, reduce risks, and improve overall performance. It also facilitates decision-making, enhances coordination, and increases efficiency.

questions.q: What is the difference between planning and decision-making? Explain.
questions.answer: Planning involves selecting the best course of action to achieve a specific goal, whereas decision-making involves choosing the most suitable option from available alternatives. Planning is a broader concept that encompasses decision-making.

questions.q: State any four limitations of planning.
questions.answer: The four limitations of planning are: (i) planning is based on forecasts which may not always be accurate, (ii) planning involves a lot of time and effort, (iii) planning may not be flexible, and (iv) planning may not be suitable for dynamic environments.
